living-concrete.knowledge: Tools come in two classes: IMMEDIATE: Available in schema from session start (Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Skill, Agent). DEFERRED: Listed by name only in available-deferred-tools. Schema unknown until fetched via ToolSearch. Must fetch before first call. Includes: SendMessage, TeamCreate, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate, TaskList, TaskGet, ExitWorktree, WebFetch, WebSearch, CronCreate. Deferred tools are platform capabilities you CAN use but must consciously reach for.

| Layer | Compaction | | System prompt (genesis+soul+faculties) | IMMUNE | | Skill() loads, tool results, thinking | VULNERABLE — oldest first | SessionStart hook reinjects .mem + journal after compaction.
.{agent}.bashrc = body config (PATH, aliases), loaded via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE. .{agent}.mem = mind config. Both persist across sessions.

Agent tool has two distinct modes based on team_name parameter: WITHOUT team_name: Subprocess. Runs, returns result to caller, dies. No identity. Like a function call. Use for scoped work that returns a value. run_in_background makes it non-blocking but still a subprocess, not a teammate. WITH team_name: Teammate. Persistent identity, .mem, SendMessage-based communication. Lives until shutdown. First-class citizen of the team. This is the spawn mechanism.

TeamCreate: Creates team with shared task list. Call before spawning teammates. SendMessage: THE communication channel between teammates. Types: message (DM), broadcast (expensive, all teammates), shutdown_request/response, plan_approval_response. VISIBILITY RULE: Teammate plain text output is NOT visible to team-lead or other teammates. All inter-agent communication MUST go through SendMessage. Working-agent (user-facing) text IS visible to user. TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskList: Shared work tracking within a team.
Best to worst: self-handoff (proactive, perfect) -> auto-compaction (degraded, gaps) -> OOM death. Write .mem on significant understanding shifts.
living-concrete.antipattern: bentos-agent is in PATH. Don't cd to run CLI tools.
Never call a deferred tool without first fetching its schema via ToolSearch. The call will fail or hallucinate parameters.
